notes: task-estimate-marker for aider. The agent emits its own progress estimate in-band as a hidden HTML comment in assistant prose — <!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":3,"completed_rounds":M} --> — advanced 0→1→2→3 across four working turns, and irrlicht surfaces task_estimate {total_rounds:3, completed_rounds:M, updated_at} + a monotone-non-increasing task_completion_eta on the session metrics WHILE working, then drops both fields at ready. DAEMON NOW HANDLES THIS (was known_failing before commit 1ae457d9): the aider adapter now scans the marker. closeModelCall calls tailer.ScanTaskEstimate(text, time.Now()) on the FULL accumulated prose buffer BEFORE truncate drops all but the last 200 display runes, and sets ParsedEvent.TaskEstimate (aider/parser.go:176-188); the shared scan lives in core/pkg/tailer/taskestimate_scan.go. Unit tests cover prose-parse (TestParser_TaskEstimate_FromAssistantProse) and the truncation trap (TestParser_TaskEstimate_SurvivesLongTurn, marker behind 40 filler lines) through ParseLineRaw. The marker survives in the transcript: an HTML comment in assistant prose is neither a #### user line nor a > blockquote, so ParseLineRaw buffers it as ordinary prose (parser.go:135-143) and lands in .aider.chat.history.md. The downstream pipeline (tailer persistence → domain TaskEstimate/TaskCompletionEta projection at elapsed/completed → API metrics, suppressed off the working state) was already in place and is shared with claudecode. The spec asserts the correct (claudecode-equivalent) behavior; this is no longer known_failing on account of the daemon. EMISSION RISK (provisional): recorded setup uses gpt-5-nano via ~/.aider.conf.yml; aider's terse diff-focused style + a small model make stable marker emission uncertain (the 2026-05-31 claudecode experiment measured 0% exact-format compliance, hence the tolerant scan) — record promotes the live emission from provisional to settled. SESSION IDENTITY (aider-specific): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD, RawLineParser), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> — no separate transcript-UUID session, no handoff/reconcile; every event (session_birth, pid_discovered, the turn arc, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, chained off session_birth via same_session_as. session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh, tmux REPL), NOT headless --message: aider emits no end-of-turn marker, so working→ready is IdleFlush-synthesized (~1.5s quiet window clamped to the ~5s SessionDetector poll); keeping aider alive across four send+wait_turn rounds plus a ≥10s trailing idle lets the daemon observe each working span and the final ready settle (where the estimate fields must drop) before the single tmux kill-session teardown. PREREQS (not daemon): recording needs a provider key (none in this environment; rig delegates auth to the user) and a one-time human seed of ~/.aider.conf.yml `read: <persistent CONVENTIONS.md>` carrying the marker rule (survives into the run because run-cell.sh does not isolate the agent's $HOME). This expected.jsonl is the documented target the recording validates against once those prereqs are satisfied.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch (UNPINNED anchor so a transient proc-<PID> presession row can't steal the birth)
PID bound within 1s — proc-<PID> session for the whole run (FilesUnderCWD, no transcript-UUID session)

Round-1 prompt → working. The agent emits the first marker (completed_rounds:0/total_rounds:3); EXPECTED: task_estimate.total_rounds=3, task_estimate.completed_rounds=0 present on the working session's metrics (the aider scan now produces task_estimate).
Round-1 turn settles to ready (IdleFlush). The session is no longer working, so task_estimate/task_completion_eta are suppressed here — they reappear on the next working turn.

Round-2 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:1. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=1 (≤ total_rounds, non-decreasing vs round 1), updated_at advances, and task_completion_eta is present and ≤ its round-1 value (ETA burns down).

Round-3 prompt → working; marker advances to completed_rounds:2. EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=2 (non-decreasing), updated_at advances, task_completion_eta ≤ the round-2 value (continued burndown).
Final working turn → marker reaches completed_rounds:3 == total_rounds (the agent reports the task done). EXPECTED: task_estimate.completed_rounds=3=total_rounds on the working metrics.
After a ≥10s trailing idle the session settles to ready. EXPECTED: task_estimate AND task_completion_eta are ABSENT once the session is no longer working (suppressed-by-state).
REPL torn down once after the final settle → a single process_exited on the one proc-<PID> session; state immediately before teardown is ready. Final state: ready.
